title: Providing amounts for the expenses of the Committee on Armed Services in the One Hundred Nineteenth Congress.
plain_summary: This resolution gives money to the House Committee on Armed Services to pay for their work during the 119th Congress. The committee will get almost twenty-six million dollars total. The money is split in half between the two sessions of Congress. All spending must follow House rules and get proper approval. This money helps the committee do their job of overseeing military and defense issues.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on House Administration.

sections.title: Section 1  Amounts for Committee Expenses
sections.text: The Committee on Armed Services will be given money to pay for its work during the One Hundred Nineteenth Congress. This includes paying the salaries of all staff members who work for the committee. The total amount of money that can be spent is no more than twenty-five million nine hundred seventy-seven thousand and seventy dollars. This money will come from the House of Representatives accounts that are set aside for committee salaries and expenses.

sections.title: Section 2  Session Limitations
sections.text: The money mentioned in Section 1 will be split into two parts. No more than twelve million nine hundred eighty-eight thousand five hundred thirty-five dollars can be spent during the first session. This first session runs from noon on January third two thousand twenty-five until right before noon on January third two thousand twenty-six. The same amount of money, no more than twelve million nine hundred eighty-eight thousand five hundred thirty-five dollars, can be spent during the second session. The second session runs from noon on January third two thousand twenty-six until right before noon on January third two thousand twenty-seven.

sections.title: Section 3  Vouchers
sections.text: To get paid under this resolution, the committee must use special forms called vouchers. These vouchers must be approved by the Committee on Armed Services and signed by the Chairman of that committee. The Committee on House Administration must also approve these vouchers in whatever way they require.

sections.title: Section 4  Regulations
sections.text: All money given under this resolution must be spent according to the rules set by the Committee on House Administration.
